name: Ecuador Organic Law on Personal Data Protection
summary: Ecuador's Organic Law on Personal Data Protection is the country's first comprehensive data privacy law, closely modeled on the GDPR. It establishes principles-based data processing rules, individual rights, and cross-border transfer mechanisms, with enforcement by the Data Protection Authority under the Telecommunications Ministry.
keyRequirements: Lawful basis for processing including consent, contractual necessity, legal obligation, and legitimate interest
Data subject rights including access, rectification, erasure, portability, and objection
Data Protection Impact Assessments for high-risk processing
Data Protection Officer appointment for public entities and certain private organizations
Cross-border data transfer restrictions with adequacy or appropriate safeguards requirements
Data breach notification to the supervisory authority within 72 hours
penalties: Fines up to 1% of annual turnover for serious violations. Administrative sanctions including temporary or definitive suspension of data processing activities. The Data Protection Authority may conduct audits and issue corrective orders.
